# PRD10 COST-575 Constant Currency Requirements

# Goal:

Give customers a way to define stable exchange rates.

# Who is asking for it?

Part of the Sovereign Cloud initiative.

Also: Silicon Valley Bank, Elm, NTT Data, Deloitte and others.

# Who is it for and how will they be engaged?

# What is it?

The current implementation of currency in Cost Management fetches a daily exchange rate from the Open Exchange Rates public API (free tier), then applies that to the current billing period (also to previous cost reports back to the maximum retention period? TBC). Usually, that’s not how it works in the real world.

Customers with enough negotiation power agree to table exchange rates with their banks to have predictability in their results. E. g. Deloitte may agree with Morgan Stanley that the USD-EUR exchange rate to be applied for Q1 2026 will be 1 USD \= 0.87 EUR and that’s how much Deloitte will pay to Morgan Stanley for that period of time, no matter what the actual exchange rate in the stock exchange will be.

For stable (pairs of) currencies (eg. Swiss Franc to Euro), rates may be agreed for longer periods of time (eg. 1 year).

For unstable currencies (eg. Nigerian Naira, that can go up or down 20% per quarter), rates may be agreed for shorter periods of time (eg per month, or per week), or not agreed at all.

# How will it work?

For the purpose of this PRD and first implementation, we are going to assume currency exchange rates will be defined for a minimum of 1 billing period (natural month) or more, or simply not defined at all (so we will need to use the last of the month exchange rate). We might need to allow shorter periods of time in the future, though (some mockups show that).

## UI

In the UI the user can select a supported currency from the dropdown the same as today, the only difference is on the backend that the underlined conversion rates for this selection would be configured by the user.

In the Settings page, we currently have a “Currency and calculations” screen:

![][image1]
We will add a new “Other” (or some other name) tab at the end, after “Platform projects”.

We will rename “Currency and calculations” to “Currency” and move the “Show cost as (Amazon Web Services only)” section to the “Other” tab.

![][image2]

In the “Currency” tab, we will rename the “Currency” header to “Display currency”.

* Have a note at the top of this page explaining dynamic currency API will be used if there is no user defined currency set.

We will add the currency exchange rate table displaying the existing currency exchange parts after “Display currency”. New currency exchange rates will be added by clicking an “Add currency pair”.

* Add exchange rate button
* Edit currency (Pencil icon, in table)
* Remove (Minus button, in table)

![][image3]

## Currency scope

Currency exchange rates will be defined per tenant (org\_id in the case of Cost Management SaaS, or Cost Management on-prem tenant when the service tenancy model will be defined in [COST-7102](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/COST-7102)). Different tenants in the same Cost Management instance may have different exchange rates.

For the purpose of this PRD, we can simplify validity periods to be monthly-based: currency exchange rates will be scoped to a minimum of 1 natural month (billing month. Example: 2026-01-01 \- 2026-01-31 (validation needs to be added to ensure user is limited to first and last day of month only, no midmonth selection)

## Dynamic currency exchange rate

Undefined currency exchange rate pairs will still use dynamic currency exchange rates. When the billing period (natural month) is finalized, we will use the last of the month's exchange rate and store it for that month, so that the cost report for a finalized period of time does not change afterwards.

A gap period in a static currency exchange rate amounts to undefined currency exchange rate, therefore falls back to dynamic currency exchange rate. E. g. Let’s say the USD-EUR currency pair is statically from January 1, 2026  to March 31, 2026 and from May 1, 2026to Jul 31, 2026, but not from Apr 1, 2026to Apr 30, 2026\. This gap amounts to an undefined static rate from Apr 1 to Apr 30, therefore a dynamic currency exchange rate is used.

## Static currency exchange rate

We will let users define a currency1-to-currency2 exchange rate table, for any pair of currencies supported by Cost Management.

By default, the same conversion rate will be used in both directions (currency1 to currency2, and currency2 to currency1), unless the reserve conversion rate exists in the table (in that case, we would use different exchange rates).

There will be a table displaying the existing currency exchange rates and allowing Price List Administrator users to edit them.

### Multi-hop conversion is not supported

Let’s say there’s a static EUR-USD rate defined and a USD-CNY rate defined but no EUR-CNY rate. For now, we will NOT support multi-hop conversion enabling EUR-CNY conversion.

Supporting multi-hop conversion could lead to complex prioritization problems where multiple same-length conversion paths are possible. E. g. EUR-USD and USD-CNY are defined, and EUR-JPY and JPY-CNY are defined. What pair should we use to convert EUR to CNY? Through USD or through JPY? Let’s not waste cycles solving that problem now. If customers demand this feature, we will gather ideas from them.

## Enabling currencies

Currencies available for static and dynamic exchange must be explicitly enabled by a Cost Management Administrator in the Settings.

For dynamic exchange rates, Cost Management will fetch the list of currencies supported by the API but they will arrive in Cost Management as disabled currencies.

## Currency exchange rate on premise

### Dynamic

~~Cost Management on premise will also support and make use of the Open Exchange Rates API, free tier, for dynamic currency exchange conversions. We will not implement support for the paid tiers of Open ER API in this PRD. That will be part of a future PRD, if ever needed.~~

We agreed to make the URL a variable, and give them the example in the documentation of the API we use in production today.

### Static

In the case of fully airgapped, isolated, disconnected customers (i. .e customers with absolutely no access to [open.er-api.com](http://open.er-api.com)), Cost Management will only support and use “bring your own currency exchange", i.e. if an exchange rate between two currencies has been defined in the table, then those two currencies will be available in the dropdowns, API, etc.

Additionally, any dynamic exchange rate imported from an exchange rate API for an enabled currency will be supported in dropdowns, API, etc.

In case there's no API URL and no static exchange rate table, or all of the currencies are disabled, either hide the dropdown or show a message like "no exchange rates available", whatever is easier.

### Corner case: no exchange rate

One corner case is where only static exchange rates can be used but no exchange rate has been defined, eg:

* Cloud bill comes in one currency (eg. USD)
* Static currency exchange rate defines conversion rates amongst other pairs (e.g. EUR-CHF and CNY-SAR)
* Customers want to see their costs in EUR but we have no way of doing that because there’s no exchange rate between USD and EUR.

In this case, we have two alternatives:

1) Do not make EUR, CHF, CNY, SAR available because there’s no exchange rates between them are available
2) (PREFERRED) Make EUR, CHF, CNY, SAR available but show an error after the user chooses a target currency that’s not available: “no exchange rate available, ask your administrator to configure static exchange rates or enable dynamic exchange rates”.

This corner case can also present itself in other incarnations, without clouds being involved, and we should act the same. Eg:

* OpenShift price list has been defined in USD
* No exchange rate has been defined in the from USD to EUR
* Customer wants to see costs in EUR

## Lifecycle of exchange rates

When defining a currency conversion rate, a validity period for that exchange rate (with start and end dates) must be defined too, similar to how we defined a validity period for the lifecycle of price lists. Validity period overlaps for the same pair, in the same direction, are not allowed.

Same as price lists, exchange rates are automatically (auto increment) versioned, the history of an exchange rate can be viewed, and an older version can be restored (as a new version).

Currency exchange rates can also be copied.

Technical detail: in case a name is needed for the currency exchange rate, we will automatically create it as “currency1-currency2”. Users cannot edit this name. (We should consider Base and Target currency naming over currency1, currency2)

Exchange rates need no description.

## Adjustments (Phase 2\)

When a currency exchange rate is updated (and that included adding or removing an exchange rate), we will use the same logic we defined for the lifecycle of price lists:

* If the exchange rate is modified within its validity period and we are in the billing period, the new rate is applied, no adjustment is generated.
* If the exchange rate is modified within its validity period but it affects out-of-billing period cost reports, an adjustment will be generated, a Price List Administrator will need to accept it before it’s applied.

## Cost reporting enhancements

When displaying or exporting a cost report that mixes different currencies, we will show what the exchange rate for each currency is.

When multiple exchange rates were used for the same currency (e.g. it's a cost report for 3 months and the currency exchange rates were defined monthly), we will display all of them and what period of time each one was used for.

## Examples and Scenarios

## Happy path

Let’s say:

1. Today is February 28, 2026
2. Mr Customer has created an AWS account in the USA in US dollars
3. Mr Customer has received charges in his AWS account for $1,234 USD
4. Mr Customer has defined the following exchange table:

| Currency1 | Currency2 | Rate | Start date | End date |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| USD | EUR | 0.87 | 01.01.2026 | 03.31.2026 |



5. Mr Customer receives charges in USD but wants to see them in EUR, so conversion per table is applied:
   Total charges \= 1,234 USD x 0.87 EUR/USD \= 1,073.58 EUR

Mixed currency case

Let’s say:

6. Today is February 28, 2026
7. Mr Customer has created an AWS account in the USA in US dollars and an Azure account in Germany in EUR
8. Mr Customer has received charges in his AWS account for $1,234 USD
9. Mr Customer has received charges in his Azure account for €456 EUR
10. Mr Customer has defined the following exchange table:

| Currency1 | Currency2 | Rate | Start date | End date |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| USD | EUR | 0.87 | 01.01.2026 | 03.31.2026 |



11. Mr Customer receives charges in USD but wants to see them in EUR, so conversion per table is applied:
    Total charges \= 1,234 USD x 0.87 EUR/USD \+ 456 EUR \= 1,073.58 \+ 456 EUR \= 1529.58 EUR
12. If Mr Customer wanted to see the charges in USD, we would apply the same entry in the currency exchange table, just in inverse:
    Total charges \= 1,234 USD \+ 456 EUR x (1/0.87 USD/EUR) \= 1,234 \+ 524.13 \= $1758.1379 USD

## New Features Summary

1. ~~Tenant-Scoped Exchange Rates~~
   1. ~~Exchange rates stored per tenant (org\_id) instead of globally~~
   2. ~~Different tenants can have different exchange rates~~
   3. (Decided to worry about this
2. Static (User-Defined) Exchange Rate Table
   1. UI table for Price List Administrators to define currency1-to-currency2 rates (New APIs)
   2. Support for any currency pairs supported by Cost Management
   3. Auto-generated names ("currency1-currency2")
   4. Bidirectional rates (same rate both directions, unless reverse explicitly defined)
3. Exchange Rate Validity Periods (Natural months/billing periods)
   1. Start and end dates for each exchange rate definition
4. Exchange Rate Versioning & History
   1. Automatic versioning (auto-increment)
   2. View history of exchange rate changes
   3. Restore older versions (as a new version)
5. Exchange Rate Copy Functionality
6. Finalized Period Exchange Rate Storage (Dynamic API rates only)
   1. When the billing period is complete, store the dynamic rate for the last day of the month, to ensure historical reports don't change
7. Fallback to Dynamic Rates
   1. Undefined currency pairs still use dynamic (external API) rates
   2. Hybrid system: static where defined, dynamic where not
8. Adjustment Workflow for Retroactive Changes
   1. Changes within billing period: apply immediately, no adjustment
   2. Changes affecting past periods: generate adjustment requiring admin approval
9. Enhanced Cost Report Display
   1. Show exchange rate used for each currency in reports
   2. When multiple rates used (e.g., 3-month rage using different currencies each month), display the correct conversion rate per month items

### Phase 1: The "Core Exchange" (MVP)

1. ~~Tenant-Scoped Rates: Necessary because every company has different bank deals.~~
   1. Decided to worry about this functionality in a separate issue
2. Static Rate Table: The actual interface to input the data.
3. Fallback to Dynamic rates API when possible
4. Basic version number (Auto increment, any change increments version number)
5. Validity Periods (start/end month)
6. Update the UI/Exports to show exactly which currency conversion rate was used for calculations.

### Phase 2: Enterprise Grade Compliance & Audit (Future work, in step with price list lifecycles audit/adjustments system)

1. Verbose version history of all currency changes.
   1. If a bill changes, auditors need to know who changed the rate and when.
2. Add the "Clone/Copy" button in the UI. Users might want to copy "Q1 2026" rates to "Q2 2026" with one click. (including from previous versions)
3. Adjustment Workflow: Prevents "silent" changes to historical data. This is a massive safety net for the customer.
